nodes.context: Criminal organization led by Kid Cann and his brothers, involved in bootlegging, gambling, prostitution, and labor racketeering in Minneapolis.
Major organized crime syndicate based in Chicago, Illinois.
One of the Five Families of New York City's American Mafia.
Irish-American organized crime group in Minneapolis, rival to Kid Cann's organization.
Rival Jewish mob faction in Minneapolis led by David Berman, briefly eclipsed Kid Cann in the 1940s.
Las Vegas-based criminal operation run by Bugsy Siegel; Kid Cann was involved in running the El Cortez Hotel and Casino.
Miami-based criminal syndicate led by Meyer Lansky; Kid Cann and Lansky operated a real estate empire in Miami Beach.
Minneapolis criminal faction led by Tommy Banks; left largely unscathed by Humphrey's crackdown.

edges.context: The AZ Syndicate had ties to the Chicago Outfit dating back to the Prohibition period.
The AZ Syndicate had ties to the Genovese crime family dating back to the Prohibition period.
Kid Cann's rivalry with Minneapolis's Irish Mob ended after dividing the city with a handshake.
David Berman briefly eclipsed the Blumenfelds and became leader of the Minneapolis underworld during Kline's mayoral term.
Kid Cann was involved with Bugsy Siegel in running the El Cortez Hotel and Casino in Las Vegas.
Kid Cann and Meyer Lansky operated a real estate empire in Miami Beach and had financial stakes in hotels.
The Blumenfelds and Tommy Banks' operations were left largely unscathed by Humphrey's crackdown on David Berman.
